novel_features.name: Pacing drift sweep
novel_features.description: See which PMP deals drifted off pace since your last sync — one ranked view across your whole deal book.
novel_features.rationale: Diffs each deal's latest delivery/pacing snapshot against the prior snapshot in the local store; the web UI and official MCP server keep no history.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to answer 'what changed since last week' across many deals at once instead of opening each deal's reporting tab.

novel_features.name: Forecast permutation sweep
novel_features.description: Run an avails forecast across a whole targeting matrix (geo x device x audience) in one command and get the grid.
novel_features.rationale: Expands the matrix to the cartesian set and calls inventoryInsights once per cell with a cached token and throttle, persisting every result; the SPA returns one forecast per click.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when planning supply: see where avails actually exist across the whole targeting surface, not one cell at a time.
novel_features.group: Orchestration the web UI can't do

novel_features.name: Cross-entity reconciliation
novel_features.description: Find structural problems across your book: deals with no line item, line items with zero delivery, budgets that don't match delivered spend.
novel_features.rationale: Four-way local join across synced deals, campaigns, line items, and latest report rows; no single API endpoint spans them.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this before a client review to catch orphans and budget mismatches the separate web screens never surface together.

novel_features.name: Bulk deal writer with quota guard
novel_features.description: Stage a batch of deal creates/updates from a file, validate locally, dry-run by default, and commit within the daily rate caps.
novel_features.rationale: Validates each row against synced reference tables and counts writes against the documented 15-create / 500-update daily caps, stopping cleanly before the cap.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this for bulk activation or teardown so a rate-limit doesn't leave a half-applied batch mid-flight.
novel_features.group: Orchestration the web UI can't do

novel_features.name: Bid-funnel win-rate ranking
novel_features.description: Rank every deal by its bid-funnel win rate (eligible to bid to win) so the worst performers surface first.
novel_features.rationale: Loops the locally-stored deal set and ranks by computed win-rate; the single-deal funnel is one API call, but ranking needs the local deal set.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to triage which deals to troubleshoot first instead of opening funnels one by one.

narrative.headline: Every Maestro deal, avails forecast, and report — scriptable, with a local database and offline analytics the web UI and official MCP server can't match.
narrative.value_prop: A command-line interface for Equativ's Maestro curation platform. It mirrors the full deal, media-planning, reporting, and activation surface, then adds what a stateless API mirror can't: a local SQLite store with sync and full-text search, pacing-drift detection (deals drift), avails permutation sweeps (forecast sweep), cross-entity reconciliation (reconcile), and quota-aware bulk deal writes (deals apply).
narrative.auth_narrative: Maestro uses OAuth2 client-credentials. Create an API User in Maestro to get a client_id and client_secret, set EQUATIV_CLIENT_ID and EQUATIV_CLIENT_SECRET, then run 'equativ-maestro-pp-cli auth login'. The CLI exchanges them at login.eqtv.io for a short-lived Bearer token and caches it, refreshing automatically before expiry. Set EQUATIV_MAESTRO_BASE_URL to override the API host (default buyerconnectapis.smartadserver.com).

quickstart.comment: Check config and reachability before anything else (works without credentials).
Pull your deals into the local store so search and analytics work offline.
See which deals drifted off pace since the last sync.

troubleshoots.fix: Run 'equativ-maestro-pp-cli auth login' to mint a fresh token; the Bearer token is short-lived (per the server's expires_in) and the CLI auto-refreshes it before expiry.
The API caps deal creations at 15/day and updates at 500/day. Use 'deals apply' which counts against the cap and stops before breaching it.
Set EQUATIV_MAESTRO_BASE_URL=https://demand-api.eqtv.io if your API User is provisioned on the documented Demand API host instead of the default live host.
drift needs at least two syncs to compare. Run 'sync --resources deals' now and again later, then re-run drift.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI to script Equativ Maestro: bulk deal operations, avails forecasting across many targeting permutations, pulling delivery/pacing reports for offline analysis, and reconciling a curation book. It shines for agency traders, media planners, and ad-ops who live in spreadsheets and want the Maestro surface as composable commands with a local database.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for ad creative rendering or the mobile Display SDK — that is a separate Equativ product.
Do not use it for publisher/supply-side monetization (the EMP supply API) — this is the demand/buyer side only.
Do not use it as a real-time bidder; it manages deals/campaigns/reports, it does not bid.

recipes.title: Agent-friendly deal list, narrowed fields
recipes.explanation: Return only the fields an agent needs from the deal list instead of the full 43-field object.

recipes.title: Forecast avails across a targeting grid
recipes.explanation: Runs the geo x device matrix of inventoryInsights forecasts (numeric IDs from `countries`/reference data) and returns avails + auctions per cell.

recipes.title: Weekly pacing-drift triage
recipes.explanation: Rank deals whose pacing moved more than 10% since the last sync and pipe the CSV into your spreadsheet.

recipes.title: Pre-review reconciliation
recipes.explanation: Surface orphaned deals/line items and budget-vs-spend mismatches across the synced book before a client review.

recipes.title: Dry-run a bulk deal batch
recipes.explanation: Validate and preview every create/update locally; nothing is written until you add --commit.

trigger_phrases: which Maestro deals are off pace
